core_mechanism: a non-equilibrium absorbing-state phase transition driven by competition between a spreading/branching process and a decay/annihilation process, whose critical universality class is continuously tuned by a discretization (time-step) control parameter interpolating between two distinct universality classes
brief_reason: The abstract describes a competition between a coherent 'infection' (spreading) process and a dissipative 'recovery' (decay) process undergoing a phase transition, and shows that discretizing the dynamics constructs a systematic continuous interpolation between distinct universality classes.
